articles.title: Replication and compartmentalization of Dobrava-Belgrade virus in the central nervous system.
Hantavirus seroprevalence and associated factors for exposure in south-central Uganda.
Hantavirus disease and the need for pathogenesis-guided therapy.
Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship kills three.
Hantavirus infections: an emerging zoonotic threat in the context of ecological change.
Epidemiological Investigation and Analysis of Orthohantavirus hantanense and Orthohantavirus seoulense From Wild Rodents in Liaoning Province, China.
Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome complicated by reversible secondary hyperparathyroidism during renal recovery: a case report.
Diagnostic and Clinical Challenges of Hantavirus-Associated Acute Kidney Injury.
Emerging and re-emerging vector-borne and other zoonotic RNA viruses: pathogenesis, climate-driven dynamics, and strategies for global control.
Characterization of the gut microbiome of wild Peromyscus sonoriensis in New Mexico, USA.
MV Hondius arrives and anchors at Tenerife as hantavirus evacuation gets underway
articles.summary: CNN confirms MV Hondius has arrived and anchored at Tenerife port. The ship's position inside the port was deemed safest by Spanish authorities. Passengers are disembarking by nationality in inflatable boats as the evacuation operation gets underway. None of the 147 aboard are symptomatic. Countries sending repatriation aircraft include Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, the Netherlands, the United States (Nebraska NQU), the United Kingdom, and the EU (two planes). WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is personally overseeing operations from Tenerife.

articles.title: Hantavirus ship evacuation underway: passengers leaving MV Hondius by inflatable boat at Tenerife
articles.summary: FTN News reports the evacuation of MV Hondius is now actively underway at Tenerife. Passengers are being ferried ashore in inflatable/tender boats, grouped by nationality, and transported in sealed guarded vehicles to Tenerife South Airport for their respective repatriation flights. The operation is proceeding within the approved weather window — Sunday through Monday — before sea state deteriorates. Authorities confirm none of the 147 aboard show symptoms. The operation is the largest coordinated multi-national hantavirus response evacuation in history.

articles.title: Protests in Tenerife as hantavirus ship MV Hondius arrives at Canary Islands port
articles.summary: NBC News and MSN report mass protests in Tenerife as MV Hondius arrived at the Canary Islands. Residents took to the streets opposing the ship's arrival, reflecting widespread anxiety about the hantavirus outbreak despite reassurances from WHO Director-General Tedros and Spanish health authorities that public risk remains low. Protesters voiced concerns that authorities were not being transparent about safety protocols. Police maintained perimeters around the port operation. The Spanish government overrode local objections, citing international humanitarian law obligations to the 147 people aboard.

articles.title: MV Hondius passengers to be quarantined after hantavirus outbreak — Madrid court authorises mandatory isolation
articles.summary: A Madrid court authorised mandatory isolation for 14 Spanish passengers upon the ship's arrival in Tenerife. The passengers will be admitted to Gómez Ulla Hospital's UATAN high-level isolation unit for a minimum of seven days for medical evaluation and monitoring. The hospital prepared to hire 60–80 extra staff for the operation. WHO's Maria Van Kerkhove cautioned that widespread media coverage may be amplifying public perception of danger beyond the actual risk level. The evacuation is expected to require approximately 10 flights to repatriate all passengers.

articles.title: CDC playing smaller role in MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak as structural changes limit response
articles.summary: STAT News reports the CDC's response to the MV Hondius outbreak is constrained by structural changes under the Trump administration. Thousands of CDC scientists and public health professionals have been laid off, including members of the ship sanitation program. The administration's withdrawal from WHO has restricted CDC scientists from communicating with international counterparts. CDC acting director Jay Bhattacharya told Fox News 'please don't worry' but subsequently provided inaccurate details about the outbreak. CDC deployed teams to Spain and Nebraska but WHO is leading the international response. The US is building bilateral health agreements with individual countries instead of working through multilateral organisations.

articles.title: CDC says threat of widespread outbreak of hantavirus remains low
articles.summary: NPR reports the CDC has deep experience with the Andes strain of hantavirus and assures the American public that the risk for a widespread outbreak remains extremely low. Person-to-person transmission is rare. More than two dozen American passengers were aboard MV Hondius: seven have already returned to the US and are under state monitoring, while 17 remain aboard for repatriation to the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center. The Dutch index couple is thought to have contracted the virus during a birdwatching excursion at an Argentine landfill site.

articles.title: WHO director arrives in Canary Islands to oversee hantavirus cruise evacuation: 'This disease is not COVID'
articles.summary: CBS News reports WHO Director-General Tedros arrived in Tenerife on Saturday to oversee the evacuation of more than 100 passengers. Tedros emphasised: 'This disease is not COVID. The risk to the local population is low.' He acknowledged pandemic trauma persists — 'That trauma is still in our minds' — but stressed the biological distinction between hantavirus and coronavirus transmission patterns. WHO estimates approximately 10 total evacuation flights: six to EU countries, four to non-EU nations. WHO recommends each country isolate evacuated passengers for 42 days from last exposure. 17 Americans to be taken to the National Quarantine Center at University of Nebraska.

articles.title: Aboard the hantavirus-hit ship, passengers fear what awaits back home
articles.summary: 1 News NZ reports from aboard MV Hondius, providing rare first-person passenger perspectives. Passengers describe relatively calm daily life: reading, attending talks wearing masks, early morning exercise on upper decks, and wildlife photography. A Spanish passenger expressed alarm at social media hostility: 'You go onto social media — they want to dynamite the boat. They want to sink the boat.' Another described heading toward Tenerife: 'You see what's out there and you realise you're heading into the eye of a hurricane.' Passengers' primary fear is not the virus itself but social stigma and public ostracisation upon arrival.

articles.title: Spain detects third hantavirus 'contact' — 45-year-old Catalonian resident identified via seat-switch tracing
articles.summary: The Spanish Eye reports a third hantavirus contact has been identified in Spain: a 45-year-old Catalonian resident who was on the same flight as the deceased MV Hondius passenger. She was initially missed during contact tracing because she changed seats during the flight, complicating manifest-based reconstruction. She is asymptomatic and under monitoring — not hospitalised. Spain's total flight-linked contacts now stand at three: (1) 32-year-old Alicante woman hospitalised with mild symptoms, PCR pending; (2) Barcelona-based woman missed due to seat-switch, asymptomatic; (3) 45-year-old Catalonian, asymptomatic.

articles.title: I've investigated a hantavirus outbreak. Here's what I can tell you about the cruise ship cluster
articles.summary: Craig Dalton, Conjoint Associate Professor at the University of Newcastle's School of Medicine and Public Health, draws on his experience investigating the 1993 Sin Nombre virus outbreak in the southwestern US. He notes the first MV Hondius case had a five-day symptom onset — shorter than the typical incubation period — suggesting pre-boarding exposure in Argentina rather than onboard infection. Later cases create ambiguity: they could represent common environmental source exposure during shore excursions or limited human-to-human transmission, requiring disciplined epidemiological investigation to distinguish between hypotheses. WHO currently assesses global risk as low.

articles.title: CDC Health Advisory Notice HAN-00528: 2026 Multi-country Andes virus cluster linked to cruise ship
articles.summary: CDC Health Advisory formally directed at US physicians and health departments (published May 8, distributed May 9). Instructs clinicians to include hantavirus pulmonary syndrome in differential diagnosis for any patient with compatible symptoms AND epidemiological risk factors within 42 days of symptom onset. Symptoms: early phase (fever, fatigue, myalgia), late phase (coughing, shortness of breath, chest tightness) 4–10 days later. Infection control: airborne isolation room, gown, gloves, eye protection, N95 or higher respirator. As of May 8: 8 cases (6 confirmed, 2 suspected), 3 deaths. Risk to US public 'extremely low'.

articles.title: Message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response
articles.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus publishes a formal public message directly addressed to the residents of Tenerife. Tedros acknowledges their anxiety — including memories of the COVID pandemic — and states clearly: 'This is not another COVID. The current public health risk from hantavirus remains low.' He notes there are no symptomatic passengers on the ship, a WHO expert is on board, medical supplies are in place, and Spain has prepared a detailed disembarkation plan with sealed vehicles through a cordoned corridor away from residential areas. Tedros announces he will personally travel to Tenerife to observe the operation firsthand. This is an unusually direct DG-level communication to a single local community.

articles.title: WHO chief meets Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez before flying to Tenerife to oversee disembarkation
articles.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros met Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez at the PM's official residence (Moncloa) at 15:00 GMT on Saturday May 9, before travelling to Tenerife. Tedros then accompanied Spain's health and interior ministers to a joint command post in Tenerife to coordinate the MV Hondius disembarkation operation. The meeting represents head-of-state level engagement in the hantavirus response — rare for an outbreak assessed as low global risk — and signals the political weight Spain and WHO are attaching to the evacuation logistics. Sources: EWN, The Journal.ie, Channels Television, FMT, BSS News.

articles.title: Updates on Andes virus (hantavirus) outbreak on the cruise ship MV Hondius
articles.summary: The Dutch Ministry of Foreign Affairs publishes the Netherlands' first formal public weblog on the MV Hondius outbreak — significant as the Netherlands is the ship's flag state. The ministry confirms it is coordinating assistance via the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in close contact with RIVM, the Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport, Oceanwide Expeditions, and authorities in Cape Verde and affected countries. A team from the Dutch embassy in Madrid and Rapid Deployment Consular Support Team (SCOT) staff are being deployed to Tenerife to receive Dutch nationals safely and coordinate repatriation. The ministry facilitated earlier medical evacuations in cooperation with Dutch embassies in the region.

articles.title: Questions and answers on the hantavirus outbreak in a cruise ship
articles.summary: ECDC publishes a dedicated Q&A page on the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak aimed at the general public and health professionals. Key Q&As cover: what Andes virus is; why it is different from common hantaviruses; the low risk to the European population; current working hypothesis that a passenger was exposed in Argentina/Chile before boarding; why human-to-human transmission requires prolonged close contact (unlike COVID-19); and what ECDC is doing to support member states. Complements the ECDC ongoing-updates page and the Technical Assessment Brief (TAB, May 6). Published as a distinct communication product from the May 9 ongoing-updates surveillance landing page already in tracker.

articles.title: Hantavirus: Current Situation
articles.summary: CDC launches a dedicated 'Current Situation' summary page for the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak. The page centralises CDC's situational awareness content: outbreak timeline, US passenger monitoring status (six states, 9+ individuals, all asymptomatic), CDC deployment details (teams at Canary Islands and Nebraska NQU), and risk assessment for the American public (extremely low). Serves as CDC's public-facing single-source hub for the outbreak, separate from CDC Newsroom press releases and the Health Alert Network advisory.

articles.title: CDC Provides Update on Hantavirus Outbreak Linked to M/V Hondius Cruise Ship
articles.summary: CDC issues a second official press release updating the public on the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak. The release confirms CDC has deployed a team of epidemiologists and medical professionals to the Canary Islands to conduct an exposure risk assessment for each American passenger upon arrival and provide monitoring recommendations. An additional CDC team has been deployed to support the National Quarantine Unit (NQU) at Offutt AFB / University of Nebraska Medical Center for returning American passengers. Risk to the American public remains extremely low. No confirmed US hantavirus cases linked to the cruise ship. Follows the first CDC statement (first press release, earlier date).

articles.title: CDC issues Health Alert Network (HAN) advisory alerting US physicians to potential imported hantavirus cases
articles.summary: CDC issues a Health Alert Network (HAN) advisory directed at US physicians and health departments. The advisory reminds clinicians to consider hantavirus infection if a patient presents with compatible symptoms (fever, myalgia, progressive respiratory distress) combined with a known travel or contact history consistent with potential Andes virus exposure. The advisory specifies standard infection control precautions: isolated room, gown, gloves, eye protection, N95 or higher respirator. The HAN advisory is a formal channel for CDC to alert the US clinical community to potential imported infectious disease cases. No confirmed hantavirus cases in the US at time of publication. Sources: ABC News live updates, Yahoo News live updates.

articles.title: As hantavirus outbreak ship heads toward Canary Islands, locals protest
articles.summary: CBS News video report showing residents of Tenerife and Canary Islands dock workers protesting the approaching MV Hondius. Protesters blow whistles, sound vuvuzelas, and carry banners outside the Canary Islands parliament building in Santa Cruz. Port workers demand special safety measures and adequate information about health protocols before an infected vessel arrives. The protest video captures the local anxiety that prompted WHO Director-General Tedros to publish his direct message to Tenerife residents and personally travel to the island.

articles.title: Spain's Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
articles.summary: Al Jazeera May 9 report as the Canary Islands prepare for the arrival of MV Hondius. Covers local protest atmosphere, Spanish government preparations, and WHO's involvement. The anti-establishment group Iustitia Europa called for the ship to be barred, posting: 'The Canary Islands cannot become Europe's health laboratory … We demand transparency, responsibility, and protection for Spaniards.' The Spanish government overrode local objections citing international humanitarian law. Ship expected to arrive in early hours of Sunday May 10; evacuation planned by nationality groups.

articles.title: The hantavirus ship is almost here: anger rises in Tenerife — is it dangerous for residents?
articles.summary: Euronews May 9 feature examining local anger and public health risk in Tenerife. Profiles dock worker protests — port workers gathered outside Canary Islands parliament with whistles, vuvuzelas, and banners voicing concern about being allowed to work without special safety measures or information. Euronews frames the key public question: is MV Hondius's arrival actually dangerous for Tenerife residents? Health experts note Andes virus does not spread via casual contact and no symptomatic passengers remain on board; WHO assesses public risk as low. Spanish government and WHO coordination highlighted.

articles.title: MV Hondius evacuation: passengers disembarked by nationality in inflatable boats, tender operations begin noon Sunday
articles.summary: Italian news agency LaPresse and Live Science reporting confirm that the MV Hondius evacuation protocol calls for passengers to be disembarked by nationality in small inflatable/tender boats, with operations scheduled to begin around noon local time on Sunday May 10 — after the ship arrives in the early morning hours. Each nationality group is escorted in isolated, guarded vehicles directly to their designated repatriation aircraft on the runway. The noon start allows time for the ship's early-morning arrival (now estimated at 04:00–06:00 CEST, updated from earlier 03:00–05:00 estimate), health checks, and coordination before tender operations begin. Weather window is Sunday noon through Monday before conditions deteriorate.

articles.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country — DON600
articles.summary: WHO Disease Outbreak News update (DON600), published May 8–9 2026. Key changes from DON599: three of the previously suspected cases are now confirmed, plus one additional confirmed case reported, raising the total to 8 cases (6 confirmed, 2 probable) including 3 deaths (2 confirmed, 1 probable). Case fatality ratio 38%. All six laboratory-confirmed cases identified as Andes virus by PCR or sequencing. WHO notes that 2,500 diagnostic kits have been shipped from Argentina to laboratories in five countries to strengthen testing capacity. Working hypothesis remains that case 1 acquired infection prior to boarding through environmental exposure in Argentina/Chile, with subsequent human-to-human transmission on board. WHO risk assessment: global population risk LOW.

articles.title: Second hantavirus 'contact' declared in Spain: Woman was missed after 'switching seats' on plane carrying infected passenger
articles.summary: The Spanish Eye reports a second hantavirus contact case has been identified in Spain: a woman based in Barcelona who was aboard the same flight as the deceased MV Hondius passenger but was initially missed during contact tracing because she had switched seats during the flight, making seat-based manifest reconstruction inaccurate. She has been traced and is asymptomatic. Authorities are now monitoring her. This is the second flight-linked contact case identified in Spain after the Alicante suspected case (announced May 8). She is not currently hospitalised.

articles.title: Andes Hantavirus outbreak in cruise ship, May 2026 — ongoing updates
articles.summary: ECDC launches a dedicated ongoing-updates surveillance page for the MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak. The page aggregates technical assessments, risk communications, and surveillance data as the situation evolves. Separate from the earlier Technical Assessment Brief (TAB, May 6) and the rapid risk assessment (May 6). Intended as the single authoritative ECDC landing point for member state public health authorities and the general public seeking up-to-date information on the ship cluster.

articles.source: EWN / Manila Times / Spain in English
articles.title: WHO chief due in Canaries to coordinate hantavirus ship evacuation
articles.summary: Multiple international outlets (EWN, Manila Times, Spain in English) report that WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is travelling to Tenerife, Canary Islands on Saturday May 9 to personally coordinate the evacuation of MV Hondius. Tedros will accompany Spain's health and interior ministers to a command post to ensure coordination between administrations, health control, and the application of planned surveillance and response protocols. His visit underscores the international significance of the event. The ship is expected to arrive at Tenerife in the early hours of Sunday May 10.

articles.title: French citizen monitored as hantavirus 'contact case' after same flight as infected passenger
articles.summary: French authorities confirm a French man is being monitored as a hantavirus 'contact case' after travelling on the same commercial flight as a deceased MV Hondius passenger. The flight in question transported a Dutch woman from Saint Helena to Johannesburg; the aircraft carried 82 passengers and six crew. French health officials are tracing all passengers from that flight. The French man is asymptomatic and the case is classified as a contact under monitoring — not a suspected or confirmed case. Source: France 24, The Peninsula Qatar.

articles.title: UK plane to bring Brits home from hantavirus cruise ship with oxygen and testing kits
articles.summary: The British government has chartered a dedicated evacuation aircraft to repatriate approximately 22 British nationals from MV Hondius. The plane is equipped with oxygen and onboard testing kits to manage any symptomatic passengers during the flight. Separately, GB News reports a second Briton aboard the ship is showing hantavirus symptoms as the UK coordinates the evacuation. All returning British nationals are subject to UKHSA's 45-day mandatory isolation protocol. The UK evacuation flight is expected to depart Tenerife shortly after the ship arrives Sunday morning.

articles.title: What we don't know about the hantavirus outbreak on the cruise ship
articles.summary: PBS NewsHour analysis piece cataloguing the key open questions in the MV Hondius outbreak investigation: (1) the precise origin of the Dutch index couple's infection — Argentine investigators suspect the bird-watching trip, but no organisation has confirmed the exact exposure site; (2) the transmission delay — authorities were not notified until May 2, two weeks after passengers began disembarking, hampering early contact tracing; (3) whether the Andes virus reliably transmits between humans on a confined vessel or whether co-exposure to a common source explains secondary cases; (4) the true attack rate once all 40+ early-departing passengers are fully tested. Includes expert commentary on what surveillance gaps this outbreak has exposed.

articles.title: Cruise ship at center of hantavirus outbreak to dock in Spain Sunday
articles.summary: CIDRAP (Center for Infectious Disease Research and Policy, University of Minnesota) reports the MV Hondius is on course to arrive at Tenerife, Canary Islands on Sunday May 10. Spain has designated a completely isolated, cordoned-off arrival area. Passengers will be transferred to shore by tender boat and transported by bus to Tenerife South Airport for repatriation. CDC public health staff will meet the ship and escort American passengers to a repatriation charter flight bound for the National Quarantine Unit (NQU) in Nebraska. Coverage notes the weather window (Sunday noon to Monday) is the critical constraint for the operation.

articles.title: Alicante hantavirus PCR still pending — positive 'unlikely', second PCR to follow if negative
articles.summary: Spanish health officials state on May 9 that the first PCR result for the 32-year-old Alicante woman remains pending. Officials characterise a positive result as 'unlikely' given the brief, indirect nature of the flight contact (two rows behind the deceased passenger). Protocol: if the first PCR is negative but symptoms persist, a second PCR will be performed at 24 hours. If infection is ultimately ruled out, she will be transferred to Madrid's Gómez Ulla hospital to complete a 42-day precautionary quarantine. If positive, she will be transferred to La Fe hospital in Valencia for specialist treatment.

articles.title: MV Hondius to arrive Tenerife early Sunday May 10, weather window Sunday noon–Monday only
articles.summary: Oceanwide Expeditions' May 8 19:00 CET press update confirms MV Hondius is expected to reach Tenerife in the early morning hours of Sunday May 10, likely between 03:00 and 05:00 CEST. The tender-boat evacuation and passenger transfers to Tenerife South Airport are planned to begin after first light. Authorities note a narrow weather window: conditions are expected to be favourable Sunday noon through Monday, with worsening sea state and wind thereafter making tender operations difficult. All 146 remaining passengers and crew remain aboard and none are currently symptomatic. Evacuation must be completed before the weather deteriorates early next week.

articles.title: First suspected hantavirus case detected in Spain: woman isolates with symptoms in Alicante hospital
articles.summary: The Olive Press reports the first suspected hantavirus case on mainland Spain: a 32-year-old woman is isolated in a hospital in Alicante with mild respiratory symptoms including coughing and general malaise. She was identified through Europe's health alert system after she travelled on the same flight as a Dutch woman who had been a passenger on MV Hondius and subsequently died from the virus. The Spanish woman was seated two rows behind the deceased passenger; authorities describe the contact as brief. Spain's Secretary of State for Health Javier Padilla announced the suspected case. Results expected within 24–48 hours. Classified as SUSPECTED — not yet confirmed.

articles.title: Hantavirus case suspected in Alicante, say officials
articles.summary: RTE (Ireland's national broadcaster) reports Spain has identified a suspected hantavirus case in the Alicante region. A woman has been admitted to hospital in Alicante after developing respiratory symptoms following contact with a deceased MV Hondius passenger on a shared flight. Secretary of State for Health Javier Padilla confirmed the suspected case, noting the woman was sitting two rows behind the cruise ship passenger who later died. PCR testing is ongoing; results are expected within 24–48 hours. The case remains classified as suspected.

articles.title: Hantavirus cruise ship: Six confirmed cases and three suspected as ship sails between Cape Verde and Canary Islands
articles.summary: Sky News reports a 6th case has been lab-confirmed, raising the total to 6 confirmed and 3 suspected (9 total) linked to MV Hondius. The ship is sailing between Cape Verde and the Canary Islands en route to Tenerife. The body of the third deceased passenger remains on board. Martin Anstee, the British expedition guide hospitalised in the Netherlands (case 5), tells Sky News he is 'doing ok' but many tests remain pending. The 6th confirmation is noted as an American passenger with mild illness recovering on board.

articles.title: Actualización del Boletín Epidemiológico Nacional semana N° 16 — Hantavirus y brote en el MV Hondius
articles.summary: Argentina's Ministry of Health publishes the week 16 Boletín Epidemiológico Nacional with a special focus section on the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster. The bulletin documents the 4-month road trip of the Dutch index couple: they entered Argentina on November 27, 2025, crossed repeatedly into Chile and Uruguay, visited Neuquén (re-entering Argentina from Chile on January 31) and Misiones in northeastern Argentina, departed for Uruguay by land on March 13, returned to Argentina on March 27, and boarded MV Hondius in Ushuaia on April 1, 2026. The bulletin identifies both Neuquén (Patagonian Sur) and Misiones as established ANDV-endemic zones. Argentine investigators (ANLIS-Malbrán) are conducting rodent capture and testing along the identified travel route. The bulletin records 6 cases and 3 deaths linked to the ship cluster. Tierra del Fuego has no endemic hantavirus. Argentina is providing diagnostic reagents to affected European countries.

articles.title: Argentina MOH: Statement and Special Report on Recent Hantavirus Activity
articles.summary: Avian Flu Diary (Michael Coston) translates and summarises Argentina's official Ministry of Health statement and special hantavirus report. The report details the index couple's 4-month overland travel through Chile, Uruguay, and multiple Argentine provinces (Neuquén, Misiones) between November 27, 2025 and April 1, 2026, before boarding MV Hondius at Ushuaia. Argentine epidemiologists are prioritising rodent capture in Neuquén and Misiones given their ANDV endemicity. The MOH confirms 6 cases and 3 deaths in the ship cluster and notes that Tierra del Fuego — the embarkation province — has no recorded hantavirus transmission. The report identifies the sequence of exposure windows to be consistent with Andes virus incubation (7–39 days).

articles.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
articles.summary: NPR reports Spain is preparing to receive the 146 remaining passengers and crew of MV Hondius at Tenerife. Spanish authorities confirm an isolated, cordoned-off arrival area. All non-Spanish nationals will be repatriated directly; 14 Spanish nationals transferred to Gómez Ulla UATAN. The KLM flight attendant has tested negative for hantavirus. UKHSA confirms a third British national is a suspected case on Tristan da Cunha.

articles.title: Hantavirus: the risks, the science and what you need to know
articles.summary: NPR Short Wave science podcast episode covering hantavirus biology and the MV Hondius outbreak. Explains rodent-to-human and Andes virus human-to-human transmission mechanisms, why no licensed vaccine or antiviral exists, and the public health significance of the ship cluster. Aimed at general audiences.

articles.title: Hantavirus Outbreak On Luxury Cruise Ship: 2 Indian Crew Members Onboard
articles.summary: Two Indian nationals are part of the crew aboard MV Hondius. Neither has been identified as a confirmed or suspected hantavirus case. Oceanwide Expeditions has not disclosed their test status or proximity to infected passengers. Indian media coverage notes a gap in communication protocols between cruise operators, flag states, and workers' home countries during onboard disease outbreaks.

articles.title: Three Canadians isolating at home after hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
articles.summary: The Globe and Mail reports three Canadians are self-isolating: two Ontario residents who disembarked MV Hondius at Saint Helena on April 24 (asymptomatic, under provincial monitoring), and one Quebec resident with possible contact with an infected traveller but never aboard the ship. Four further Canadians remain aboard MV Hondius; Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand confirmed consular staff have been dispatched to Tenerife.

articles.title: Map Shows Where Hantavirus Has Spread
articles.summary: Newsweek publishes an interactive map of confirmed and suspected hantavirus cases linked to MV Hondius, covering affected countries across Europe, the Americas, Asia, and remote South Atlantic territories. Notes that 8 ship-linked cases (3 deaths) have been reported across at least 10 countries, with contact tracing active in 12+ nations.

articles.title: Deadly Hantavirus Outbreak: 5 Questions Experts are Facing
articles.summary: Newsweek frames five open questions dominating expert discussion: (1) Did human-to-human transmission occur on board? (2) How many additional cases will emerge among the ~40 early-disembarking passengers? (3) Will the Tristan da Cunha suspected case be confirmed? (4) What is the ship's exact attack rate once full passenger testing is complete? (5) Are there unreported cases in countries with limited diagnostic capacity? No new confirmed cases announced.

articles.title: Technical Assessment Brief: Hantavirus-associated cluster of illness on a cruise ship
articles.summary: ECDC formal risk assessment document on the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster. Covers epidemiological analysis, Andes virus transmission characteristics, risk to EU/EEA population, and recommendations for member states on surveillance and contact tracing.

articles.title: Suspected hantavirus on the cruise ship MV Hondius
articles.summary: Official statement from Tristan da Cunha government. MV Hondius visited the island April 13-15. No cases of hantavirus identified on Tristan da Cunha. One British national who disembarked is a suspected case under UKHSA investigation.

articles.title: New hantavirus case suspected on remote island as contact tracing continues
articles.summary: UKHSA confirms suspected hantavirus case on Tristan da Cunha, a remote British Overseas Territory in the South Atlantic. The individual is a British national who was aboard MV Hondius. Contact tracing continues across 12+ countries.

articles.title: UKHSA update on the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak
articles.summary: Two British nationals confirmed with hantavirus (one in Netherlands, one in South Africa). Third British national on Tristan da Cunha under investigation as suspected case. All returning British passengers subject to 45-day mandatory isolation with UKHSA monitoring and testing. Risk to UK general public remains very low.

articles.title: Hantavirus : premier cas suspecté hors du bateau, l'évacuation des passagers débute
articles.summary: French-language report from Première Ligne confirming the first suspected hantavirus case identified in a person outside the ship: the KLM flight attendant who briefly came into contact with a deceased MV Hondius passenger at OR Tambo International Airport, Johannesburg. Simultaneously, the video reports that passenger evacuation from MV Hondius has formally begun at Tenerife on May 9, with tender boats ferrying passengers while the ship remains at anchor offshore.

articles.title: First Person Outside Cruise Ship Is Suspected Of Hantavirus Infection
articles.summary: Health Policy Watch confirms the first hantavirus-suspected case in a person with no direct ship connection: a Dutch KLM flight attendant hospitalised at Amsterdam UMC after brief contact with the deceased second MV Hondius passenger (Dutch female, case 2) who briefly boarded KLM flight KL592 at Johannesburg on April 25. The attendant subsequently tested NEGATIVE for Andes virus by PCR and antibody testing, ruling out transmission. The article also notes that the Swiss passenger (case 8) represents the first off-ship confirmed case after disembarking at St. Helena.

articles.title: Tenerife Braces as Cruise Ship with Hantavirus Docks Amid Concerns
articles.summary: MV Hondius has arrived at Tenerife amid local anxiety. Canary Islands residents, recalling the 2020 hotel lockdown affecting 700+ tourists, expressed concern about the docking despite WHO assurances of low public risk. All 146 remaining passengers are asymptomatic and will be repatriated after medical checks; non-Spanish nationals repatriated directly, 14 Spanish nationals transferred to Gómez Ulla UATAN unit in Madrid. Tender-boat evacuation replaces direct docking after port worker blockade threat.

articles.title: Spain says hantavirus ship evacuees need new plane to leave Canaries
articles.summary: One of two medical evacuation aircraft transporting sick MV Hondius patients from Cape Verde to Amsterdam made an emergency diversion to the Canary Islands after the on-board physician reported a fault in the electrical life-support system of one patient. Spain's health ministry arranged a replacement aircraft while the patient remained on the original plane connected to the airport's power supply. The three evacuees (British case 5 Martin Anstee, Dutch crew member, German asymptomatic contact) were eventually transported to specialized facilities in the Netherlands and Germany.

articles.title: This American doctor thought he was going on vacation. He ended up treating hantavirus patients on the infected ship
articles.summary: CNN interview with an American passenger physician aboard MV Hondius who assisted in caring for infected patients during the outbreak. The physician describes the progression of the outbreak, the isolation protocols implemented on board, and the decision-making around the three medical evacuations. Provides a first-hand clinical perspective on managing Andes virus HCPS at sea with limited resources. The ship's own doctor (crew physician) was among those evacuated to Johannesburg and remains in intensive care with improving condition, according to WHO.

articles.title: Former Hantavirus Cruise Passenger Speaks from Hospital: 'I'm Doing Ok'
articles.summary: Martin Anstee, the British expedition guide and confirmed hantavirus case 5, speaks via Sky News from his isolation room in the Netherlands. He reports feeling 'not too bad' but says many tests remain pending and his doctors will have a clearer picture later in the week. He is isolated and uncertain of his discharge timeline. First direct patient statement from the MV Hondius outbreak.

articles.title: Hantavirus Ship En Route to Spain, Military Hospital Quarantine Awaits
articles.summary: Spain discloses the full health protocol for receiving MV Hondius. The 14 Spanish nationals will be transferred to the High-Level Isolation Unit (UATAN) at Hospital Central de la Defensa Gómez Ulla, Madrid — a facility built after the 2014 Ebola response and previously used for COVID-19 Wuhan evacuees. Quarantine duration: up to 45 days, calibrated to last potential exposure date. Admission is voluntary, with a consent form required.

articles.title: En la planta 22 del Gómez Ulla: así es la unidad UATAN de aislamiento donde irán los 14 españoles del hantavirus
articles.summary: El Español provides an inside description of floor 22 of the Gómez Ulla military hospital, which houses Spain's UATAN high-level isolation unit. The unit features negative-pressure rooms, airlock entries, and dedicated clinical staff trained for BSL-3/4-equivalent pathogens. It will house the 14 Spanish MV Hondius passengers for their quarantine period. The facility is Spain's most advanced biosafety isolation infrastructure.

articles.title: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Set To Dock In Tenerife As Quarantine Plan Moves Ahead
articles.summary: MV Hondius has formally requested permission from the Canary Islands Port Authority to dock at Tenerife on Saturday May 9 at 12:00 CET, though arrival may slip to Sunday. All 146 remaining passengers and crew — none symptomatic — will disembark; foreign nationals will be repatriated directly, while 14 Spanish nationals will be transferred to Gómez Ulla. Evacuation operations are planned from May 11 onwards.

articles.title: WHO officials on hantavirus cases: 'This is an outbreak on a ship, not another COVID-19'
articles.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros and officials explicitly characterise the MV Hondius cluster as a contained ship-linked outbreak, not a pandemic-scale event. Experts note that while the Andes strain can transmit between humans, it requires sustained close contact and does not possess the aerosol spread efficiency seen in SARS-CoV-2. CIDRAP coverage includes key takeaways from the WHO press briefing.

articles.title: At least 8 sickened in suspected hantavirus outbreak; Andes strain confirmed
articles.summary: CIDRAP reports confirmation of at least 8 cases linked to MV Hondius, with the Andes strain positively identified by laboratory testing. Timeline analysis suggests the Dutch index couple was likely infected during Argentine shore excursions prior to boarding, consistent with a 10-20 day incubation period. Three deaths confirmed. Ship evacuees transferred to Netherlands and Germany.

articles.title: 2 Singapore residents isolated at NCID after hantavirus exposure on cruise and flight
articles.summary: Singapore's Communicable Diseases Agency confirms two residents who were aboard MV Hondius are now isolated at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases. A 67-year-old Singaporean male arrived May 2 (asymptomatic) and a 65-year-old permanent resident male arrived May 6 (mild rhinorrhoea). Both shared an April 25 flight with a confirmed hantavirus case. Negative test followed by 30-day quarantine protocol. Public risk assessed as low.

articles.title: 5 U.S. states monitoring passengers who departed cruise ship stricken by hantavirus
articles.summary: Five US states have confirmed they are monitoring residents with potential MV Hondius exposure: Georgia (2), Texas (2), Arizona (1), Virginia (1), and California (number undisclosed). All are currently asymptomatic. Seventeen US nationals were aboard the vessel. At least 12 countries are simultaneously tracking dispersed passengers.

articles.title: 3 Canadians isolating in Ontario, Quebec after hantavirus ship outbreak
articles.summary: Canada confirms three individuals self-isolating in Ontario (2) and Quebec (1) following potential hantavirus exposure. Two are former MV Hondius passengers who disembarked at Saint Helena on April 24; both are asymptomatic and under provincial monitoring. The third, in Quebec, was not aboard the ship but is self-isolating after possible contact with an infected person during international travel — Public Health Agency of Canada assessed their risk as not high. All three are under 30-day monitoring. Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand confirmed four further Canadian nationals remain aboard MV Hondius; consular officials dispatched to the Canary Islands.

articles.title: 3 Canadians isolating in 2 provinces over possible hantavirus exposure
articles.summary: Global News reports on Canada's three self-isolating individuals: two Ontario residents who were MV Hondius passengers disembarking at Saint Helena on April 24, and one Quebec resident who had possible contact with an infected person during international travel but was never aboard the ship. Quebec's health minister confirmed the Quebec individual is not considered high-risk. All three are asymptomatic and under 30-day monitoring with symptom checks by local public health authorities.

articles.title: Key takeaways from WHO briefing on hantavirus cruise ship outbreak
articles.summary: STAT News distils the May 7 WHO press briefing: WHO expects more cases may emerge but not a large epidemic; human-to-human Andes virus transmission requires prolonged close contact; the KLM flight attendant tested negative; and approximately 40 passengers who disembarked before quarantine enforcement are being traced across 12+ countries.

articles.title: Hantavirus on the rise in Argentina, where MV Hondius cruise ship set sail
articles.summary: Argentina's hantavirus burden for 2025–2026 is running at approximately twice the baseline rate, with 101 reported cases since June 2025. The elevated endemic activity in Patagonia preceded the MV Hondius outbreak and provides context for the likely origin of infection during Ushuaia and Argentine shore excursions.

articles.title: Kiwi aboard MV Hondius cruise ship with deadly hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: New Zealand confirms at least one national remains aboard MV Hondius; another Kiwi passenger had disembarked earlier and returned home before quarantine was imposed. The New Zealand Ministry of Health is monitoring the situation and in contact with WHO. A second RNZ article confirms the on-board Kiwi has not requested consular assistance.

articles.title: Hantavirus ship to remain offshore after Tenerife port blockade threat
articles.summary: MV Hondius will not dock at Tenerife's Granadilla port after local dockworkers threatened a blockade. Canary Islands president Fernando Clavijo announced passengers will be evacuated using tender boats while the ship remains anchored offshore. The evacuation plan was revised after Spain's health minister coordinated with regional authorities.

articles.title: Two British passengers of hantavirus cruise ship self-isolating in the UK
articles.summary: Two British former MV Hondius passengers have returned independently to the UK and are self-isolating under UKHSA guidance. Neither is currently reporting symptoms. UKHSA is also supporting a small number of additional close contacts under self-isolation.

articles.title: Hantavirus outbreak: Former British police officer evacuated from rat-virus ship named after 'very traumatic few days'
articles.summary: Martin Anstee, 56, a retired British police officer serving as expedition guide on MV Hondius, is named as the British hantavirus case (case 5). He was medically evacuated on May 6 near Cape Verde and transferred to a hospital in the Netherlands for specialist treatment.

articles.title: Brit police officer seriously ill with rat virus pictured as two more self-isolate in UK after potential exposure
articles.summary: Martin Anstee, British expedition guide and retired police officer, is pictured and confirmed as the UK national hospitalised with hantavirus from MV Hondius. Two further UK-based former passengers are self-isolating following UKHSA guidance with no current symptoms.

articles.title: What the numbers tell us about hantavirus
articles.summary: CNN analysis published May 8 examining the epidemiological picture of the MV Hondius outbreak: attack rate, case fatality rate, generation interval, and what the spread pattern reveals about Andes virus human-to-human transmission dynamics. Contextualises the cluster against historical ANDV outbreaks in Argentina and Chile. Notes that while CFR among confirmed ship cases exceeds 30%, this likely reflects ascertainment bias toward severe presentations.

articles.title: Third British person with suspected hantavirus found on Tristan da Cunha
articles.summary: RTE reports a third British national has been identified with suspected hantavirus on Tristan da Cunha, a remote British Overseas Territory in the South Atlantic with a population of approximately 250. The individual was among passengers who disembarked from MV Hondius during its stop at the island in mid-April. The case is the first suspected infection among early-departing passengers and raises questions about secondary exposure in an extremely resource-limited setting.

articles.title: Update: British hantavirus cases rise to two confirmed, third suspected on Tristan da Cunha
articles.summary: Xinhua English-language report on the latest developments: UKHSA confirms two British MV Hondius passengers with confirmed hantavirus (one in Netherlands, one in South Africa) and a third suspected case on Tristan da Cunha. Report also notes the revised ship arrival date of May 10 at Tenerife and the UK's 45-day mandatory isolation order for British returnees.

articles.title: Dutch flight attendant tests negative for Andes virus
articles.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros confirms KLM flight attendant who was hospitalized after contact with a deceased MV Hondius passenger on KLM flight KL592 (Johannesburg–Amsterdam, Apr 25) has tested negative for Andes virus by both PCR and antibody testing. No flight-related transmission confirmed.

articles.title: KLM flight attendant hospitalized after contact with hantavirus cruise ship passenger
articles.summary: Dutch KLM flight attendant from Haarlem hospitalized at Amsterdam UMC with mild symptoms after working on KLM KL592 (Apr 25) on which a fatally ill MV Hondius passenger briefly boarded before being asked to disembark. Passenger subsequently died of Andes virus in Johannesburg. Flight attendant later tested negative.

articles.title: 3 evacuated from hantavirus cruise ship as Spain says it will dock in Canary Islands despite local opposition
articles.summary: Three people medically evacuated from MV Hondius on May 6: two sick crew members including the ship's doctor, and one asymptomatic German passenger with close contact history. Switzerland confirms 8th case — a male passenger now being treated at University Hospital Zurich. Spain's interior ministry overrides Canary Islands president's objections and authorises the ship to dock for evacuation from May 11.

articles.title: WHO says hantavirus cruise outbreak is 'not the next COVID', though more infections could emerge
articles.summary: Approximately 40 passengers disembarked from MV Hondius at earlier ports of call before quarantine was enforced. WHO chief Tedros states outbreak is serious but hantavirus lacks respiratory spread efficiency for pandemic potential. Health authorities across at least a dozen countries are actively tracing these passengers.

articles.title: Deadly hantavirus outbreak on ship poses 'low' risk to wider public, WHO says
articles.summary: WHO assesses global population risk as LOW. Three deaths confirmed among 8 cases linked to MV Hondius. Andes virus human-to-human transmission requires sustained close contact; experts say outbreak will not trigger a COVID-like pandemic scenario.

articles.title: A timeline of the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak and when passengers fell sick
articles.summary: Detailed chronological reconstruction of the MV Hondius outbreak from the index cases' four-month birdwatching road trip through Argentina, Chile, and Uruguay (Nov 2025–Apr 2026) to the current quarantine situation. Argentine health ministry confirmed the travel history of the Dutch couple on May 6.

articles.title: CDC classifies hantavirus outbreak as Level 3 emergency response
articles.summary: CDC has elevated the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak to a Level 3 emergency response. Live updates on ship heading to Canary Islands, contact tracing across multiple countries.

articles.title: Statement on multi-country hantavirus cluster associated with cruise ship travel
articles.summary: Africa CDC issues statement on the multi-country hantavirus cluster. Coordinates with WHO and Cape Verdean authorities on ship quarantine and regional surveillance.

articles.title: WHO confirms eighth hantavirus case linked to MV Hondius cruise ship
articles.summary: WHO confirms 8th case aboard MV Hondius. Ship now heading to Canary Islands for passenger evacuation starting May 11. 146 people from 23 countries still aboard under strict precautionary measures.

articles.title: Hantavirus cruise ship heads for Spain's Canary Islands as officials race to trace contacts
articles.summary: MV Hondius heading to Canary Islands after Cape Verde quarantine. Canary Islands president initially refused entry. Passenger evacuation to begin May 11. Spain's interior ministry coordinating.

articles.title: Cruise ship hantavirus outbreak sparks international effort to track passengers
articles.summary: US officials in Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas and Virginia monitoring 17 American passengers. Health officials in at least a dozen countries tracking dispersed passengers.

articles.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after first fatality
articles.summary: Passengers who disembarked at Saint Helena before the outbreak was identified are being traced. Some left the ship before the first death was confirmed.

articles.title: Hantavirus: What you need to know
articles.summary: BMJ clinical explainer on hantavirus following the MV Hondius outbreak. Covers Andes virus transmission, clinical presentation, and public health response.

articles.title: The Hantavirus Outbreak Is Serious. But It's No COVID, Health Officials Say
articles.summary: Health officials emphasize hantavirus lacks respiratory spread efficiency for pandemic potential. Andes virus human-to-human transmission requires close, prolonged contact.

articles.title: CDC teams will meet Americans on hantavirus-stricken ship in Canary Islands and at Nebraska quarantine unit
articles.summary: CNN reports the CDC is deploying two separate response teams for the 17 American passengers aboard MV Hondius. One team will travel to the Canary Islands to meet the ship upon arrival and escort Americans to a repatriation charter flight. A second CDC team is positioned at the National Quarantine Unit (NQU) in Nebraska — a federally funded quarantine facility co-located with the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center. All Americans returning from the ship will be placed in quarantine at the NQU. Five states (Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas, Virginia) are already monitoring seven passengers who previously disembarked, all asymptomatic.

articles.title: Hantavirus latest updates: 6 states are monitoring cruise ship passengers for potential infections
articles.summary: Yahoo News live blog update confirms New Jersey as the 6th US state monitoring residents for potential hantavirus exposure linked to MV Hondius, raising the count from 5 to 6 states. New Jersey DOH is monitoring two residents whose potential exposure occurred during air travel abroad, not directly aboard the ship. The full state breakdown: Arizona (1), California (1), Georgia (2), New Jersey (2), Texas (2), Virginia (1) — a total of at least 9 individuals across the 6 states, all currently asymptomatic. New Jersey has no history of a confirmed hantavirus case.

articles.title: NJDOH Monitoring Two New Jersey Residents Following Potential Hantavirus Exposure Linked to MV Hondius
articles.summary: Official press release from the New Jersey Department of Health confirming monitoring of two NJ residents who may have been exposed to a person infected with hantavirus who disembarked from MV Hondius. The two residents were not passengers on the cruise ship; their potential exposure occurred during international air travel. Both are asymptomatic and under active monitoring. Risk to the general public in New Jersey assessed as very low. New Jersey has no history of a confirmed hantavirus case.

articles.title: Spanish woman in hospital with suspected hantavirus infection; new case suspected on remote island
articles.summary: NBC News covers two new suspected hantavirus developments in a single report. First, a 32-year-old Spanish woman in Alicante has been hospitalised with symptoms consistent with hantavirus infection after travelling on the same flight as a deceased Dutch MV Hondius passenger — she was seated two rows behind the victim, with brief contact. Second, British authorities have confirmed a suspected hantavirus case on Tristan da Cunha, the remote South Atlantic island (population ~220) where MV Hondius made a stop on April 15. The KLM flight attendant, previously suspected, has tested negative. Experts note this is the first suspected case in both mainland Europe (flight contact) and among passengers who disembarked at an intermediate island port.

articles.title: Hantavirus update: another suspected case linked to MV Hondius as Tenerife protests intensify
articles.summary: ITIJ reports the latest MV Hondius developments for the travel insurance and assistance sector. Key update: protests have intensified outside Tenerife's parliament building, with residents and port workers chanting 'We want work, not illness' as police gathered ahead of the demonstrations. Despite the Canary Islands president's opposition, Spain's central government approved the ship's arrival citing international humanitarian law. A new suspected case linked to MV Hondius has also emerged. ITIJ notes the incident's significance for maritime travel insurance, emergency assistance protocols, and liability for cruise operators handling onboard infectious disease events.

articles.title: Hantavirus in the US: Map of States With Cruise Ship Passengers
articles.summary: Today.com publishes a US state-by-state map of residents being monitored for hantavirus exposure following the MV Hondius outbreak. Initial map shows 5 states: Texas, Georgia, Arizona, Virginia and California. Notes 17 US nationals were aboard the ship. CDC characterises risk to the American public as 'extremely low' (May 7 statement). Article contextualises the monitoring effort within broader US preparedness, noting the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska as the designated repatriation facility for returning American passengers.

articles.title: Hantavirus disease — Andes virus infection cluster associated with MV Hondius cruise ship
articles.summary: WHO publishes disease outbreak news on the hantavirus cluster associated with the MV Hondius. Seven confirmed and suspected cases with three deaths. Global population risk assessed as LOW.

articles.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship: what we know
articles.summary: Three deaths confirmed among passengers of the MV Hondius expedition cruise. Ship currently anchored off Cape Verde under quarantine.

articles.title: Health authorities trace hantavirus contacts across multiple countries
articles.summary: Contact tracing underway in the US, Spain, Singapore, Germany and Argentina for passengers who disembarked at earlier ports of call.

articles.title: Hantavirus cruise ship outbreak concerns scientists, but this is not a new pandemic
articles.summary: Infectious disease experts clarify that Andes virus human-to-human transmission is rare and hantaviruses lack the respiratory spread efficiency needed for pandemic potential.

articles.title: Rapid risk assessment: hantavirus cluster on expedition cruise ship
articles.summary: ECDC assesses risk to European population as VERY LOW. Recommends enhanced surveillance for returning passengers.

articles.title: Cruise ship quarantined off Cape Verde after hantavirus deaths
articles.summary: MV Hondius under quarantine at Praia port. Cape Verdean authorities coordinating with WHO and Dutch health authorities.

articles.title: CDC monitoring hantavirus situation, issues travel advisory for Argentina
articles.summary: CDC issues Level 2 travel health notice for Argentina regarding Andes virus risk. Enhanced screening at US ports of entry.

articles.title: First reported case of Andes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome treated with a combination of favipiravir, ribavirin, icatibant and baricitinib.
World Health Organization - Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country.
Outbreak at Sea: The MV Hondius Hantavirus Cluster as a Sentinel for Global Pandemic Readiness.
Maternal antibody-mediated elimination of a Puumala hantavirus outbreak in a bank vole colony.
When Hantavirus Sickened the Ship's Physician, Stephen Kornfeld, MD, Stepped Up.
NEJM Outbreaks Update - Andes Hantavirus.
Andes virus, hantavirus infections, and the unresolved question of transfusion transmission.
The Andes virus paradox: high lethality, low transmissibility, and the clinical urgency beyond pandemic risk.
An Unexpected Outbreak at Sea: Revisiting Hantavirus Transmission Dynamics.
Hantavirus infection: A narrative review focusing on epidemiology, diagnosis, infection control and treatment in the era of globalisation.
The MV Hondius Hantavirus Outbreak-Lessons for Pandemic Preparedness.
Human infection with Andes hantavirus: an update for the general physician.
Cruise ship hantavirus outbreak in remote island communities.
Hantavirus outbreak in a cruise departing from Argentina.
[Epidemiological surveillance of Andes virus: have we really learned anything after COVID-19?].
Hantavirus at the Hajj: Decoupling Perceived Risk from Epidemiological Reality.
Andes Hantavirus Outbreak on a Cruise Ship, 2026.
First epidemic of hantavirus on cruise ships: from passenger surveillance to ship ecology.
Hantavirus Outbreak: First Test of US Public Health Response After WHO Withdrawal.
Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard a Cruise Ship: An Urgent Wake-Up Call for Ecotourism-Related Zoonotic Risk.
Disease outbreaks such as Ebola and hantavirus are more frequent and deadly as world "moves backwards," report warns.
Hantavirus in 2026: What the MV Hondius Outbreak Demands from Transfusion Medicine.
Cruise Ships as Amplifiers of Emerging Zoonotic Threats: Implications of the Suspected Andes Hantavirus Cluster aboard MV Hondius.
Pharmaceutical Pollutants in Urban Rats Are Linked to Zoonotic Infection Risk.
[Hantavirus and pregnancy: what obstetric implications in the face of the emergence of the Andes virus?].
Glucose Dysregulation in Hantavirus Infection: A Signal Worth Watching With Continuous Glucose Monitoring.
Briefing Chat: Hantavirus - what this outbreak reveals about the disease.
Hantavirus: Quarantined British passengers are discharged to home isolation.
Hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the South Atlantic.
Hantaviruses in the One Health era: strengthening surveillance before the next spillover.
Offline: Hantavirus-surprise, complacency, and peril.
Cruise ship outbreak spotlights a little-studied hantavirus.
Hantavirus: Army drops in with medic and supplies to remote British island.
Andes hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship - an ESCMID Emerging Infections Subcommittee (EIS) rapid assessment.
Cruise-ship hantavirus cluster exposes a wider preparedness gap.
Hantavirus outbreak should reset WHO's default approach to airborne risk.
Immunoinformatics-driven design of a multi-epitope vaccine against Seoul Virus: Structural, dynamic, and immunogenic profiling.
The Coinfection of Bartonella spp. and Hantavirus in Wild Rodent and Shrew Species in Eastern China.
Genomic Analysis of Sin Nombre Virus Sequences, Northwestern United States, 2023.
Hantavirus outbreak exposes uncertainty about how disease spreads.
Hantavirus: people are betting on a pandemic breaking out.
Hantavirus: French and US nationals test positive as cruise ship docks in Canary Islands.
Identification and genetic characterization of a distinct genotype of Puumala orthohantavirus in Hebei Province, China.
When Rare Zoonoses Travel: Andes virus, Hantavirus Cardiopulmonary Syndrome, and the Preparedness Gap.
Lethal Hantavirus at Sea: Infectious Disease Outbreaks on Cruise Ships and Limits of Preparedness.
Temporal Dynamics of Viremia in Hemorrhagic Fever With Renal Syndrome Patients.
Hantavirus: Third British national has suspected infection as WHO says this is "not the start of a pandemic".
Evidence of Juquitiba and Jaborá Orthohantavirus Co-Circulation in Rodent Communities of Argentina.
There is no vaccine for deadly hantavirus: what that means for future outbreaks.
Hantavirus: What you need to know.
Hantavirus: Three dead in cruise ship outbreak as authorities investigate human-to-human transmission.
Immunopathogenic insights into members of the class Bunyaviricetes: a comparative review of emerging zoonotic threats.
Hantavirus update: Argentina (Patagonia), cruise ship cluster
articles.summary: ProMED reports third death on MV Hondius. Argentina's national health ministry confirms 101 hantavirus infections since June 2025.

articles.title: Dutch cruise company confirms hantavirus cases on expedition vessel
articles.summary: Oceanwide Expeditions confirms five suspected hantavirus cases aboard MV Hondius. Two Dutch passengers have died.

articles.title: Hantavirus-Infektionen 2026: Aktuelle Situation
articles.summary: Robert Koch Institute reports on hantavirus situation in Germany. One German national among cruise ship fatalities. Domestic Puumala virus season tracking at expected levels.

articles.title: Epidemiological update: hantavirus disease in the Americas
articles.summary: Pan American Health Organization reports elevated Andes virus activity in southern Argentina. Recommends rodent exposure prevention measures.

articles.title: Alerta epidemiologica: sindrome pulmonar por hantavirus
articles.summary: Argentina's Ministry of Health issues epidemiological alert for hantavirus pulmonary syndrome. Case counts running at twice the rate of the same period in 2024-2025.

articles.title: Hantavirus surveillance summary, United States, 2025
articles.summary: CDC publishes annual hantavirus surveillance summary. 31 HPS cases reported in 2025, consistent with long-term average.

articles.title: Connecting the region during crisis: a Community of Practice response to the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak.
Annual epidemiological report: hantavirus infection, 2024
articles.summary: ECDC publishes 2024 annual report. 2,340 confirmed cases across EU/EEA. Germany (423), Finland (312), and Sweden (198) account for majority of cases.
